title: NOC 72421 – Appliance servicers and repairers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Appliance Servicers and Repairers (NOC 72421) are the essential technical specialists ensuring the functionality of Canada’s modern, connected households. As the country moves toward a "Right to Repair" legislative framework and higher energy-efficiency standards, these professionals have transitioned from simple mechanical repair to advanced electronics and IoT (Internet of Things) diagnostics. Operating at the TEER 2 level, they service everything from smart refrigerators and induction cooktops to high-efficiency heat-pump dryers. In 2026, the sector is seeing strong demand as consumers choose to repair high-value, tech-heavy appliances rather than replace them.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Digital Diagnostics: Using 2026-grade computerized testing equipment and mobile diagnostic apps to isolate faults in electronic control boards and sensors.
Precision Repair: Disassembling appliances to repair or replace defective parts such as motors, heating elements, and intricate water-valve assemblies.
Gas & Water Integration: Connecting and testing gas-fired appliances and plumbing connections to ensure safety and compliance with the 2026 Gas and Building Codes.
Customer Service: Advising customers on the "cost-to-repair" vs. replacement, demonstrating proper use of smart features, and providing maintenance tips.
Maintenance & Cleaning: Cleaning and lubricating parts and performing routine preventive maintenance on high-efficiency laundry and kitchen systems.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school and a vocational program in Appliance Repair or Electronics is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over three years of work experience and industry courses is required for certification.
Certification: Trade certification is voluntary in most provinces, but it is highly recommended for higher wages. In Ontario, certification is through Skilled Trades Ontario.
Specialized Licensing: For technicians working on gas appliances, a Gas Fitter license (Class B or C) is mandatory in most provinces (e.g., TSSA in Ontario).
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province and access high-paying national service contracts.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Moderate to Good, with "High Hiring Difficulty" reported for technicians capable of repairing smart-circuitry and gas-integrated units:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February and March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72421 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker and Skilled Trades streams. Minimum scores for regional draws in Southwestern and Eastern Ontario started at 53, reflecting a critical need for mobile service technicians outside the major GTA core.

British Columbia: Strong demand. BC projects a high median wage of $28.50/hr, with roughly 82% of workers employed full-time. The province is actively recruiting for technicians with Gas Fitter (Class C or B) endorsements to handle modern dual-fuel kitchen ranges.
Alberta: Competitive Earning Potential. While demand is stable, Alberta offers high top-tier wages, with experienced master technicians in Calgary and Edmonton reaching $43.00/hr.

National Trend: Approximately 22% of workers in this NOC are self-employed, which is nearly double the average for other technical trades, providing a clear path for newcomers to start their own service businesses.
Why Work in Canada as an Appliance Technician?
Lead the Smart-Home Revolution: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including Wi-Fi-enabled diagnostics, AI-driven maintenance alerts, and eco-friendly R-600a refrigerant systems.
Unbeatable Job Stability: Your skills are recession-resistant. 78% of appliance technicians work all year round—significantly higher than seasonal construction trades.
High-Tier Compensation: As a TEER 2 trade, your median annual salary is approximately $61,400, with specialized technicians and small business owners in major hubs earning over $95,000.
Priority for PR: In 2026, this NOC remains a core target for the Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category and is a priority for the OINP REDI Pilot draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72421 with a score of 53+ (Southwestern/Eastern Ontario). This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Updated for 2026. Eligible candidates now require 12 months of full-time work experience (increased from 6 months) within the last 3 years to receive an ITA at lower CRS thresholds.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Appliance technicians are classified as "High-Priority Essential Service Workers."
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for technicians with a job offer from a BC-based appliance service center or manufacturer (e.g., Samsung, LG, Whirlpool service partners).
